source_jobs.title: Senior+ Backend Software Engineer - Asia
source_jobs.description:
About Palmstreet:

Palmstreet http://palmstreet.app/ is the #1 live shopping platform for rare plants, handmade crafts, & unique goods. Founded in 2020 by Instagram and Apple veterans, Palmstreet began as a plant identification app and quickly evolved into a trailblazer in live shopping, beginning with plant sellers. Fueled by a passionate, plant-loving community, we rapidly expanded into new categories to better serve our fast-growing audience.

Our vision is to become the “World’s Online Main Street,” bringing the charm of in-person shopping to engaging, social online experiences. Backed by top Silicon Valley VCs and powered by a nimble, empathetic team, we’ve grown our GMV by 500x in the last 18 months.

As we continue shaping the future of social commerce, we’re looking for talented individuals who are excited to grow with us and join our mission.



The Opportunity:

Palmstreet is looking for a Senior Software Engineer to join the team. As a Senior Software Engineer, you will lead the team on key architectural decisions, work on core user experiences like instant bidding and live interactions, develop scalable architecture to handle millions of users, innovate core features like live streaming and checkout processes, and maintain a reliable deployment pipeline.



Responsibilities:

 - Build and maintain robust backend services and APIs that power both web and mobile applications using Node.js

 - Work closely with PM and design to build scalable products and services

 - Establish self as an owner of a particular component, feature, or system with expert end-to-end understanding

 - Successfully complete projects at large scope while maintaining a consistently high level of productivity

 - Take active ownership of DevOps responsibilities including CI/CD pipelines, infrastructure management, deployment automation, and system reliability

 - Leverage AI coding tools (Cursor, Codex, Claude Code) to accelerate development velocity and improve code quality



Qualifications:

 - BS (or higher) in Computer Science

 - Bilingual proficiency in English and Mandarin

 - 5+ years experience building highly scalable and reliable infrastructure

 - Payment system experience preferred

 - Hands-on DevOps experience: CI/CD, infrastructure as code, containerization (Kubernetes, Docker), and cloud platforms (GCP/AWS)

 - Proficiency with AI-assisted development tools (Cursor, Codex, Claude Code) preferred

 - Experience building high-concurrency systems preferred

Preferred Qualifications:

 - Experience creating web interfaces using React, HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to integrate with backend services



Your Location:

 - Remote - Anywhere in the China



Compensation:

 - Salary range: 600k - 1M RMB / year.

 - 5 insurances + 1 housing fund(五险一金) included

 - Equity grants will also be provided as part of the total compensation package.



Why Join Us?

 - Shape the future of live shopping: Be part of a dynamic team revolutionizing the online shopping experience, combining creativity and technology to create immersive, interactive platforms for sellers and buyers alike.

 - Collaborate with industry pioneers: Work alongside a nimble, innovative team, backed by some of the most prestigious VCs in Silicon Valley, with opportunities to influence the product direction.

 - Solving complex challenges: You'll take on complex problems that others haven't yet been able to solve, driving innovation and pushing boundaries in your work.

 - Thrive in a flat, startup environment: Take full ownership of your projects while working alongside a supportive team that values collaboration and mutual respect.

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion:

Palmstreet is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and encourage applications from everyone—especially those whose identities are traditionally underrepresented in tech. We do not discriminate on race, religion, color, gender expression or identity, sexual orientation, ancest

source_jobs.title: General Interest (Future Roles)
source_jobs.description:
About Palmstreet:

Palmsteet https://palmstreet.app is a community-obsessed live shopping platform connecting over 2 million passionate collectors and sellers. Starting with plants in 2020, we've evolved to include handmade crafts, collectible toys, and unique finds from talented sellers worldwide.

Backed by top Silicon Valley VCs (a16z, Craft Ventures, Headline), we're building the 'World's Online Main Street'—bringing authentic personal connections and the thrill of discovering one-of-a-kind treasures to social online experiences.

With explosive growth over the past 24 months, we're scaling rapidly and looking for exceptional talent to join us in shaping the future of social commerce.



Why Join Us?

 - Shape the future of live shopping: Be part of a dynamic team revolutionizing the online shopping experience, combining creativity and technology to create immersive, interactive platforms for sellers and buyers alike.

 - Collaborate with industry pioneers: Work alongside a nimble, innovative team, backed by some of the most prestigious VCs in Silicon Valley, with opportunities to influence the product direction.

 - Solving complex challenges: You'll take on complex problems that others haven't yet been able to solve, driving innovation and pushing boundaries in your work.

 - Thrive in a flat, startup environment: Take full ownership of your projects while working alongside a supportive team that values collaboration and mutual respect.



Are you interested in what Palmstreet is building and wish to be considered for future relevant opportunities? Please apply as we are always looking forward to meeting with more great people!



Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

Palmstreet is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and encourage applications from everyone—especially those whose identities are traditionally underrepresented in tech. We do not discriminate on race, religion, color, gender expression or identity, sexual orientation, ancestry, national origin, citizenship, age, marital status, veteran status, disability status, political belief, or any other characteristic protected by law.

For roles in Canada, we also comply with the Employment Equity Act.

source_jobs.title: Business Development Manager – Collectibles Category
source_jobs.description:
About Palmstreet:

Palmstreet http://palmstreet.app/ is the #1 live shopping platform for rare plants, handmade crafts, collectibles, and unique goods. Founded in 2020 by Instagram and Apple veterans, Palmstreet began as a plant identification app and quickly evolved into a trailblazer in live shopping, beginning with plant sellers. Fueled by a passionate community, we rapidly expanded into new categories. Collectibles, launched in 2025, is one of our fastest-growing categories.

Our vision is to become the "World's Online Main Street," bringing the charm of in-person shopping to engaging, social online experiences. Backed by top Silicon Valley VCs and powered by a nimble, empathetic team, we've grown our GMV by 1500x in the last 3 years.

As we continue shaping the future of social commerce, we're looking for talented individuals who are excited to grow with us and join our mission.



The Opportunity:

Palmstreet is seeking a motivated and entrepreneurial Business Development Manager to help take our Collectibles category to the next level. A year after launch, Collectibles is already our fastest-growing category and is becoming an industry-leading player. We're now looking for a builder who can scale our existing core subcategories (toys, Disney, trading cards) while identifying and launching the next big opportunities.

This is a high-impact opportunity to shape the future of social commerce by acquiring and growing top sellers through strategic outreach, onboarding, coaching, and early-stage relationship building. You'll partner directly with the Head of Collectibles to set category direction, prioritize where we go next, and own the execution that gets us there.

This is a remote role reporting to the Head of Collectibles and working closely with the Palmstreet GTM, Seller Success/Onboarding, and Marketing teams. There is a preference for a Los Angeles-based professional who is willing to travel 25% (trade shows, seller visits, and team off-sites), but open to other locations for the right candidate.



Responsibilities:

This is a full-time role that blends strategic category work with high-output seller engagement and acquisition. Expect to spend your time across the following focus areas:

Category Growth and Seller Recruitment

 - Build and execute strategy to scale existing collectibles subcategories

 - Recruit and activate high-potential sellers

 - Reach out to new leads through outbound emails, social media, calls, and visits

 - Develop and deepen relationships with strategic sellers through coaching and tactical support

 - Represent Palmstreet at industry events and within collector communities

Category Strategy & Expansion

 - Partner with the Head of Collectibles to set strategy across core and emerging subcategories

 - Identify and test new category opportunities using a mix of market data, seller signal, and buyer behavior

 - Lead GTM activities for promising new subcategories: source initial sellers, create the marketing strategy, set early benchmarks, and decide whether to invest deeper or move on

 - Translate learnings into recommendations on category structure, marketing investment, and product needs

 - Maintain a clear point of view on competitive positioning across all categories of collectibles

Seller Acquisition & Activation

 - Pitch Palmstreet through discovery and closing calls (virtual and in-person) and onboard new sellers from first contact through their debut livestream

 - Manage a pipeline of prospective sellers across the collectibles ecosystem, from established category-leaders to emerging sellers with category-building potential

 - Balance high-touch onboarding for strategic accounts with scaled support for inbound or early-stage sellers

Revenue & Category Growth Ownership

 - Hit clear performance goals:
   
   - GMV growth across your cohort of strategic sellers/subcategories
   
   - Number of new sellers acquired, onboarded, and live
   
   - New buyer growth and retention

source_jobs.title: Senior+ Backend Software Engineer - Canada
source_jobs.description:
About Palmstreet:

Palmstreet http://palmstreet.app/ is the #1 live shopping platform for rare plants, handmade crafts, & unique goods. Founded in 2020 by Instagram and Apple veterans, Palmstreet began as a plant identification app and quickly evolved into a trailblazer in live shopping, beginning with plant sellers. Fueled by a passionate, plant-loving community, we rapidly expanded into new categories to better serve our fast-growing audience.

Our vision is to become the “World’s Online Main Street,” bringing the charm of in-person shopping to engaging, social online experiences. Backed by top Silicon Valley VCs and powered by a nimble, empathetic team, we’ve grown our GMV by 500x in the last 18 months.

As we continue shaping the future of social commerce, we’re looking for talented individuals who are excited to grow with us and join our mission.



The Opportunity:

Palmstreet is looking for a Senior Backend Software Engineer to join the team. As a Senior Software Engineer, you will lead the team on key architectural decisions, work on core user experiences like instant bidding and live interactions, develop scalable architecture to handle millions of users, innovate core features like live streaming and checkout processes, and maintain a reliable deployment pipeline.



Responsibilities:

- Build and maintain robust backend services and APIs that power both web and mobile applications using Node.js and TypeScript

- Work closely with PM, design, and infrastructure teams to build scalable products and services

- Establish self as an owner of a particular component, feature, or system with expert end-to-end understanding.

- Successfully completes projects at large scope while maintaining a consistently high level of productivity



Qualifications:

- BS (or higher) in Computer Science

- Bilingual proficiency in English and Mandarin

- 5+ years experience of building highly scalable and reliable infrastructure

- Payment system experience preferred

- Kubernetes experience preferred



Your Location:

- Remote - Anywhere in the Canada



Compensation:

- Pay Range: $200,000 - $250,000 CAD

- Equity grants will also be provided as part of the total compensation package.



Why Join Us?

- Shape the future of live shopping: Be part of a dynamic team revolutionizing the online shopping experience, combining creativity and technology to create immersive, interactive platforms for sellers and buyers alike.

- Collaborate with industry pioneers: Work alongside a nimble, innovative team, backed by some of the most prestigious VCs in Silicon Valley, with opportunities to influence the product direction.

- Solving complex challenges: You'll take on complex problems that others haven't yet been able to solve, driving innovation and pushing boundaries in your work.

- Thrive in a flat, startup environment: Take full ownership of your projects while working alongside a supportive team that values collaboration and mutual respect.

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion:

Palmstreet is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and encourage applications from everyone—especially those whose identities are traditionally underrepresented in tech. We do not discriminate on race, religion, color, gender expression or identity, sexual orientation, ancestry, national origin, citizenship, age, marital status, veteran status, disability status, political belief, or any other characteristic protected by law.

For roles in Canada, we also comply with the Employment Equity Act.

Application Process:

We’re excited to review your application! If your experience aligns with what we’re looking for, our hiring team will reach out to schedule an initial screening call and walk you through the next steps of our interview process.

While we try our best to respond to everyone, the high volume of applications may mean we’re unable to provide individual updates if we decide not to move forward.

To stay connected and explore

notes: Live shopping platform with strong engineering presence
